steps.title: Find the timing clue
steps.content: The passage states that swords first appeared in the Middle Bronze Age (approximately 14th–10th BCE). This tells us that hoards from that period or later could contain sword evidence, while earlier hoards would not.

steps.title: Apply the clue to the choices
steps.content: Choice D fits: it places sword evidence in the later Hollingbourne hoard (10th century BCE) and says none was found in the earlier Arreton Down hoard (17th century BCE). The other three either misplace the evidence, reverse the relationship, or add unsupported details about discovery dates. That matches choice D — D is correct.
